title: Vera Bradley Reports Major Revenue Drop: Q3 Sales Plunge 30% Amid Brand Repositioning Efforts
text:
Vera Bradley Q3 FY2025 Earnings Summary

Financial Results
Vera Bradley reported significant challenges in Q3 fiscal year 2025. The company generated consolidated net revenues of $80.6 million, representing a sharp decline from $115.0 million in the prior year, a 29.9% decrease.

The bottom line was notably negative. The company posted a net loss of $12.8 million, or $0.46 per diluted share, compared to net income of $5.1 million ($0.16 per share) in the same period last year.

Year-to-date through nine months, consolidated revenues reached $272.0 million versus $337.5 million previously, with accumulated losses of $15.2 million or $0.52 per diluted share.

Segment Performance
All three business segments experienced weakness:
- Vera Bradley Direct: Revenues fell 27.4% to $52.5 million
- Vera Bradley Indirect: Revenues declined 27.9% to $18.0 million
- Pura Vida: Revenues plummeted 42.9% to $10.1 million

Strategic Context
CEO Jackie Ardrey acknowledged the difficulty: The third quarter was extremely challenging as the company remained in the early stages of Project Restoration, our strategic initiative to transform our business model and transition Vera Bradley's brand positioning.

However, management expressed optimism about emerging improvements in Q4, citing steady trendline improvement and strong customer response to heritage prints.

Financial Position
Despite operational losses, Vera Bradley maintains no debt and holds $13.7 million in cash with approximately $89 million total liquidity.
